studies.feature_name: Pulsed-source fast-muonium reaction kinetics (truncated TF Mu: shared-amplitude λ_Mu → k_Mu → Arrhenius E_a)
references: Muonium reaction 2026.docx (ISIS EMU muon-school teaching guide)
D. C. Walker, Muon and Muonium Chemistry (CUP), Ch. 8
Percival, Roduner, Fischer et al., Chem. Phys. Lett. (1977)
Ng, Walker et al., J. Phys. Chem. 85, 454 (1981)
Ghandi et al., Phys. Chem. Chem. Phys. 4, 586 (2002)
studies.notes: IMPLEMENTED 2026-06-16 (Option B, signed off). core/fitting/mu_kinetics.py: fit_mu_relaxation_series (shared-A_Mu global fit over the 2 G series — globals A_1[=A_Mu]+phase, frequency fixed=f_mu, locals Lambda_2[=λ_Mu],A_3,Lambda_3; wraps fit_global on 'Oscillatory*Exponential+Exponential'), fit_bimolecular_rate (weighted-LSQ λ=λ0+k_Mu·[x]), fit_arrhenius (weighted-LSQ on the guide's log10 k vs 1/T → E_a, slope=−E/(ln10·R), default kJ/mol), mu_relaxation_from_amplitude (single-run cross-check: λ_Mu with A_Mu FIXED to a reference = analytic limit). All exported from asymmetry.core.fitting + cookbook recipe + docs/user_guide/muonium_kinetics.rst (closes DG-C6). KEY IMPLEMENTATION FINDING: the shared-amplitude fit needs ≥1 slow, well-surviving member (the deox-water [x]=0 run) to PIN A_Mu — a {quarter,half,full}-only triplet has no good anchor and the fit is under-determined (negative slope / χ²r≫1); adding the water reference fixes it. Encoded in the env-gated real-corpus test + documented as an .. important:: in the guide. VERIFIED: 8 synthetic planted-truth tests (simulate.py, no binary fixtures) pass deterministically in ~3.5s (degeneracy reproduced with share_amplitude=False → fast-member λ error ≫ shared; shared fit recovers planted λ_Mu/k_Mu/E_a; amplitude-inversion cross-check agrees). REAL CORPUS (env-gated ASYMMETRY_MALEIC_DIR, EMU 78251-78302): room-T line k_Mu=0.68±0.02 µs⁻¹/rel-conc, λ0=0.63 µs⁻¹, all four λ_Mu finite+monotonic (half/full no longer rail = the S5 ❌→✅ flip); Arrhenius 278/298/338 K → E_a=10.5±0.6 kJ/mol (diffusion-controlled order; lit ≈17.6). χ²r is high (13-28) — single-frequency model approximates the 4-line Mu⁰ TF spectrum + diamagnetic precession; slope/E_a robust regardless. k_Mu absolute not gradeable (no stock molarity → relative units only, GT §9). ---- ORIGINAL STUDY NOTE: closes the only S5 API ❌ (wimda-corpus windows-api maleic-acid kinetics; doc-gap DG-C6). Closes the only S5 API ❌ (wimda-corpus windows-api maleic-acid kinetics; doc-gap DG-C6). Gap: at a pulsed source the 2 G TF Mu signal decays before first_good_bin (t_g≈0.203 µs, EMU 78251-78302), so per-run A_Mu & λ_Mu are degenerate (A_Mu·exp(−λ·t_g) conserved) and a free fit rails — only water (λ≈0.47) and quarter (λ≈2.87) fit; half/full diverge; integrate_run is flat (TF oscillation averages to zero). Physical key: A_Mu(0) is the beam/solvent Mu fraction, common across the concentration/T series (FFT corroborates: water Mu amp 62.9 vs maleic ~12-14 = same A_Mu, different survival); maleic changes only λ_Mu. Rescue: SHARE A_Mu (+φ; f_Mu fixed) across the series, λ_Mu local — slow members pin A_Mu, which then forces λ for the truncated fast members. Seam is COMPOSITION of implemented parts: fit_global (asymmetry_global.py:174 shared globals/locals) + 'Oscillatory*Exponential+Exponential' (or MuoniumTF*Exponential) + Linear (λ=λ0+k_Mu·[x]) + built-in Arrhenius/log10-2.3R (E_a). MISSING: (1) encoded shared-A_Mu recipe; (2) discoverable kinetics entry point (series→λ_Mu→k_Mu/λ0→E_a); (3) concentration axis ([x] not in .nxs — caller-supplied vector); (4) amplitude/fraction inversion cross-check; (5) user-guide page. RECOMMENDED Option B: thin Qt-free core/fitting/mu_kinetics.py (fit_mu_relaxation_series + fit_bimolecular_rate + fit_arrhenius) + Option-C amplitude inversion as RED-test cross-check + docs. Reference programs ship NO turnkey fast-Mu kinetics workflow and NO fit log for this example (verification is synthetic-planted-truth-led + env-gated real-corpus physics check; no binary fixtures). CAVEAT: $WIMDA_SRC/$MUSRFIT_SRC/$MANTID_SRC not checked out locally — comparison rests on the teaching docx (read), Asymmetry's prior porting studies, and the muon-chemistry literature; WiMDA/Mantid muon-school recipe needs source re-trace before contracts harden. Arrhenius unit note: built-in component uses per-particle k_B; emit molar E_a (kJ/mol) via the guide's log10 k vs 1/T linear form (slope=−E/2.3R). k_Mu absolute not gradeable (no stock molarity) → relative-conc units + λ0 only.

PROPOSES REVERSING the standing exclusion in wimda-parity-gap/decision-record.md §1 ('HDF4 .nxs — Out, coverage boundary'). New grounds: HDF4 is WiMDA's native on-disk format (direct parity/usability); pyhdf is a pure-Python optional dep with wheels (asymmetry[hdf4] extra, like h5py); and the in-corpus nxs4to5 reader (hdf4tree.py+v1_to_v2.py) already reads all 1,913 corpus HDF4 files. KEY FINDING: the gap is the CONTAINER not the schema — NexusLoader._detect_layout/_load_v1 already parse the v1 /run muonTD schema; only h5py.File() fails on the HDF4 container (magic 0e031301). RECOMMENDED Option B: port hdf4tree.read_tree+is_hdf4 into core/io/hdf4.py, wrap in a thin h5py-compatible adapter (__contains__/__getitem__/keys/get/attrs/asarray) and branch load() on the HDF4 magic to feed the existing _load_v1 unchanged — reuses both Asymmetry's v1 reader and the corpus HDF4 reader; lighter than porting full v1→v2 (Option A) and avoids duplicating _load_v1 (Option C). Cross-format .nxs_v2/.RAW explicitly NOT a goal. Ready-made oracle: corpus ships every HDF4 file beside its converted data_hdf5/ twin → direct parity test. Write/export to v2 deferred. Surfaced by API session-5 (TRSB ships HDF4).

studies.feature_name: RF-µSR resonance: GUI surface (time-domain/scan picker + RF field-difference scan)
references: McKenzie et al., J. Phys. Chem. B 117, 13614 (2013)
studies.notes: GUI-surfacing of the already-implemented core RFResonanceMuP (slug rf-musr-resonance-fit, parameter_models.py field-scope component). Round-2 GUI finding: RFResonanceMuP is absent from the time-domain/ALC fit picker and there is no RF field-difference (Red-Green) scan control, so PC1 is unreachable from the GUI. Scope: register the model in the fit-function picker and add an RF period-difference scan path analogous to the integral-scan (ALC) flow.

references: Pratt et al., Phys. Rev. B 79, 052508 (2009)
Sonier et al., Rev. Mod. Phys. 72, 769 (2000)
studies.notes: Adds the field-dependent vortex-lattice line width sigma(B0) = sigma0(lambda)*(1-b)[1+1.21(1-sqrt(b))^3]/2.21, b=B0/Bc2 (Brandt PRB 37,2349/68,054506), fitting lambda_ab (nm) and Bc2 (T) for a type-II superconductor. Core: brandt_field_width_sigma + brandt_field_factor in sc/models.py, reusing sc.constants.lambda_nm_to_sigma_us (the b->0 limit). Registered field-scope components SC_Brandt_VortexLattice and SC_Brandt_VortexLattice_Powder (3^(1/4) ab-plane powder average). Optional sigma_bg adds in quadrature (Pratt Eq.2). x=field in gauss, Bc2 in tesla. 11 tests; powder B_rms plateau reproduces Pratt 2009 Fig.1 (195 nm -> 1.91 mT, 244 nm -> 1.22 mT).

Ergonomics/discoverability port of an existing internal capability: FitEngine.global_fit already does asymmetry-domain simultaneous least-squares (concatenates ds.asymmetry/ds.error, iminuit LeastSquares, shared globals + per-dataset locals); the count-domain fit_grouped_* family wraps it with a Cash factory. Gap was no first-class asymmetry-domain entry point and an undiscoverable contract (datasets must carry unique run_number; runless datasets collide on key 0). IMPLEMENTED (Option A): fit_global + GlobalFitResult in asymmetry_global.py wrap engine.global_fit with synthetic keying (sequence->positional, mapping->keys), single-ParameterSet broadcast, boundary validation (disjoint global/local, names present, finite positive errors), and a combined reduced chi2 (Sigma chi2_d over Sigma N_d - N_free_global - Sigma N_free_local_d). No minimiser duplication. Asymmetry-domain LSQ is the convenience default; count-domain Poisson/Cash remains statistically preferred for low counts (documented). Wizard left untouched (recommend vs execute separated).

studies.feature_name: Asymmetry statistical error: exact Poisson vs Mantid independent-propagation
studies.notes: Follow-on from the simulate-mode verification suite (verification §2). compute_asymmetry shipped Mantid AsymmetryCalc's independent-N/D error (1+A²)/(F+B), which over-estimates σ_A by (1+A²)/(1-A²) vs exact Poisson 4α²FB(F+B)/(F+αB)⁴ = (1-A²)/(F+B). Derivation verified analytically + Monte Carlo; oracle: Mantid is the outlier, WiMDA/musrfit/textbook use 1-A². Measured fit impact: amplitude σ +3.2%, χ²ᵣ biased low. IMPLEMENTED (Option A): compute_asymmetry now uses 2|α|√(FB(F+B))/(F+αB)² with one-sided F·B=0 bins kept at the 1.0 no-information sentinel. Tests updated: test_transforms √2->1.0 pin (renamed) + 3 new exact-form unit tests; TestRefitRecovery chi2r re-centred on 1.0. Other 'recompute' tests derive expected error by calling compute_asymmetry and stayed green. validate green (2000 passed). compute_asymmetry_with_count_errors and the simulate builder already used the exact form.

Follow-on from link-groups (PR #27). IMPLEMENTED: faithful ports of WiMDA TFMuonium/LowTFMuonium/ZFmuonium as undamped components MuoniumTF/MuoniumLowTF/MuoniumZF (composed with *Exponential like Oscillatory). Conventions: leading amplitude A (standard chain-amplitude), hyperfine A_hf (avoids the A clash), ZF anisotropy D_mu (bare D is a Redfield param), field in Gauss via existing g_mu/g_e constants, phase in radians, central diamagnetic line a separate OscillatoryField. TWO findings: (1) positive-frequency convention — TFMuonium uses signed w12 (lower satellite at -phase) while LowTFMuonium negates it; the port uses |w| so all lines share +phase. (2) These do NOT meet a CdS chi2~1.3 bar (chi2~22 vs 1.35 for independent lines) because (field,A_hf) is over-determined and the chi2 landscape is razor-sharp; WiMDA's own docx routes CdS to three independent lines = link groups. So scope = GENERAL genuine-muonium feature, verified by self-consistency (recover A_hf at chi2~1) + WiMDA arithmetic, NOT the CdS corpus. CdS stays with link groups.

WiMDA link groups are equality-only (p[follower]:=p[main]); 4 groups; main=first non-fixed member; followers drop out of the free set. No offset/linear tie exists in WiMDA. The CdS docx 'Use Ties to keep the lines equally spaced' is loose wording: equal spacing comes either from the muonium model (ZFmuonium/TFMuonium: splitting is one parameter) or from three free frequencies recovered from the data. Chosen port: equality link groups + free frequencies (matches WiMDA, no offset ties), with the improvement that a follower reports the propagated (=main's) uncertainty. Follow-on: a MuoniumTriplet model component that enforces symmetry via a single splitting parameter.

WiMDA-first implementation complete. musrfit comparison pass done 2026-05-15. Phase family investigation done 2026-05-15. Dangerous naming collisions documented: musrfit ‘phase’ = WiMDA ‘Phase Spectrum’; musrfit ‘real’ = WiMDA ‘Phase’; musrfit ‘power’ = |F|^2 != WiMDA ‘(Power)^1/2’ = |F|. Phase family conclusion: musrfit uses linear c0+c1 ONLY inside phaseOptReal optimizer; all manual/CLI paths use constant c0 only. Do NOT expose c1 as a user control; implement as optimizer backend only. t0_offset_us already covers the physical timing-slope case. Recommended follow-on: (1) phaseOptReal mode with entropy optimizer using c0+c1 backend, (2) N0 normalization for single-histogram inputs, (3) range-based background subtraction, (4) Tesla unit, (5) imag/real+imag output modes. musrfit qualitative apodization levels explicitly rejected in favour of WiMDA explicit tau/start. Per-detector FFT deferred.

Promotes candidate slug maxent-spectrum. Study pass done 2026-06-05. Four implementations found across references, three distinct methods: (1) MULTIMAX lineage = WiMDA Wimdamax.pas AND Mantid MuonMaxent/MaxentTools (same FORTRAN ancestor, near-identical — implement ONCE, never offer as separate user choices); (2) Mantid generic MaxEnt-v1 (Skilling-Bryan, PosNeg entropy, complex images — genuinely different but Mantid's muon GUI dropped it in v3.12; do not expose as second engine, keep core seam engine-shaped); (3) WiMDA src/MaxEnt.pas is Burg all-poles MEM, NOT MaxEnt — excluded, naming hazard. musrfit has NO MaxEnt (roadmap item only; its PFTPhaseCorrection entropy code is FFT phase correction). WARNING: WiMDA's own FEATURE_MAP/SYMBOL_MAP 'maxent-spectrum' entries point at the Burg code and miss Wimdamax.pas entirely — do not navigate by them. Mantid MaxentTools is GPL-3: usable as verification oracle, must not be copied into MIT Asymmetry. Decision: port MULTIMAX with WiMDA as behavioral contract, Mantid numpy port as oracle.

studies.feature_name: Theory library expansion (Keren, Abragam, Bessel, SpinGlass, Meier, MuoniumDecouplingCurve, vortex lattice)
Simulate mode (synthetic dataset generation + degrade statistics)
studies.notes: Promoted from candidate (tier now, score 16) under the wimda-parity-gap umbrella, Wave A project 8. Candidate docs preserved at docs/porting/simulate-mode/candidate/ (same-slug promotion: the structural harness requires unique slugs, so the candidates/ entry is replaced rather than kept, unlike maxent-spectrum->maxent). Study pass done 2026-06-10 against WiMDA Simulate.pas + DegradeStats.pas (Pascal read directly; brief verified). Decisions (Ben, 2026-06-10): loaded-run template required; persistence via saved NeXus file (no schema change); degrade -> new derived run (not in-place); minimal standalone NeXus V1 writer (not template-copy; works for PSI .bin templates); dialog seeds model+params from current fit else builder; core takes per-group signal specs, v1 dialog is F/B alpha-split; deadtimes written as zeros + optional flat background (default 0). Key divergences from WiMDA (all documented in comparison.md): Poisson draws of expected counts with numpy PCG64 + first-class seed (WiMDA: unseeded Delphi RNG); pre-t0 bins = background only (WiMDA: unphysical growing exponential); degrade = exact binomial thinning for f<1 (WiMDA: Poisson(k*f), over-dispersed); exact time_zero (WiMDA: quantised to whole microseconds). Double-pulse halving, count-mode simulation, two-period output deferred to count-domain-fit-modes. IMPLEMENTED 2026-06-10 (4 phases, validate green, 53 new tests): core/simulate.py (simulate_run, simulate_run_from_group_signals, degrade_run, expected_counts, reduce_run_to_dataset, promoted archetype builders), core/io/nexus_writer.py (write_nexus_v1, works for PSI .bin templates), SimulateDialog + Data Browser Degrade Statistics action + provenance badging, docs/user_guide/simulation.rst. Verified: round-trip refit recovery, 100-seed pull distribution ~ N(0,1), degrade 1/sqrt(f) error scaling, corpus round trips (nickel HDF5 + EuO PSI .bin). FINDING for follow-up: compute_asymmetry's error formula treats F+-aB as independent ((1+A^2) form), over-estimating sigma_A by (1+A^2)/(1-A^2) vs exact Poisson propagation — chi2_r centres below 1 against known truth; affects all fits; recorded as follow-on in implementation-options.md.
Promoted to full study: see slug 'maxent' (docs/porting/maxent). Candidate docs retained for scoring history; the study supersedes their technical content.
Promoted to full study: see slug 'rrf' (docs/porting/rrf). Candidate docs retained for scoring history; the study supersedes their technical content.

studies.feature_name: Python user functions (registration facade + discovery, replacing WiMDA plugin DLLs)
studies.notes: Wave B project of wimda-parity-gap; promoted from candidate (python-user-functions-candidate retained for scoring history). IMPLEMENTED 2026-06-12, 5 milestones. Facade core/fitting/user_functions.py (register_component + register_parameter_component; MODELS internal-only, models._register aligned onto shared core/fitting/registration.py); N4 naming = grammar-atom names + required domain + cross-registry uniqueness (W17 user flag exempts docs tests); discovery core/plugins.py (~/.asymmetry/user_functions + asymmetry.user_functions entry points, explicit load_user_functions(), structured report, never crashes); W1 named zero-valued placeholder degrade replaces fit_panel silent fallback; GUI: startup hook, log lines, Setup dialog, picker/info badges; Keren example plugin bit-for-bit + exact-fit parity, doubles as docs/user_guide/user_functions.rst tutorial. Design-only: getresults derived-quantities hook, sandboxing (WiMDA trust model documented), hot reload.

studies.feature_name: Muonium-radical hyperfine fit functions and workflow
BPP parametric model for muon-diffusion analysis
Dynamic relaxation functions (dynamic Kubo-Toyabe Gaussian/Lorentzian, Keren, Abragam)

studies.notes: Moves the GUI 'RG box' (Red/Green/G-R/G+R) into core and adds scriptable per-period access. Realises the 'period-arithmetic' candidate (red/green selection slice). Validated against photo-uSR silicon runs 103277-103298.

IMPLEMENTED in nexus.py (_load_v1/_load_v2): adds field_state (TF/LF/ZF raw) + detector_orientation (instrument axis) metadata; field_direction repurposed to the user-facing geometry derived ONLY from field_state (words: Transverse/Longitudinal/Zero field), blank when absent, NO orientation fallback. GUI: run-info dialog 'Detector Orientation' row + selectable browser column. Tests in test_nexus_loader.py; verified vs real corpus files. KEY FINDING (study): none of Mantid/musrfit/WiMDA reads sample/magnetic_field_state for geometry. Mantid derives main_field_direction from detector orientation (same source Asymmetry uses) but names it as the instrument axis and uses it only for default MUSR/CHRONUS grouping, not fitting. musrfit stores free-text Setup/Orientation + numeric field (informational); user declares TF/LF/ZF in .msr THEORY. WiMDA: user picks grouping/mode; reads field value + ISIS a_selected_magnet L/T/A for logbook only. Asymmetry's bug is a labelling conflation: it derives field_direction from detector orientation and presents it as the experiment geometry. Ground truth corpus (2050 pulsedTD files): 1731 TF runs mislabelled Longitudinal (orientation=L); MUSR00044991 is TF at 0 G (do NOT infer ZF from field magnitude); no 'ZF' string appears in any file; 137 EMU files lack magnetic_field_state (need orientation fallback). RECOMMENDATION (Option C, refines candidate fix): keep orientation as a separate detector_orientation field, add field_state from magnetic_field_state, user-facing geometry comes from field_state else None/unknown (decision 2026-06-07: NO orientation fallback - it would reintroduce the conflation; affects 137 files = all ARGUS + EMU nickel set + all PSI/HDF4), never synthesise ZF. NeXus NXmuon spec not available locally - confirm enum before relying on edge wording.

studies.feature_name: Time-integral asymmetry / field-scan observable (ALC / repolarisation / QLCR)
studies.notes: CORE API IMPLEMENTED (Qt-free; GUI deferred) in src/asymmetry/core/transform/integral.py, tests in tests/test_time_integral_asymmetry.py. Per-run reduction: integrate_asymmetry(forward,backward,...,method) [low-level on grouped counts, 'integral'=sum-counts-then-(F-aB)/(F+aB) and 'differential'=per-bin-asymmetry-then-mean], integrate_curve(time,asym,err) [mean of an already-formed curve e.g. combined G-R], integrate_run(dataset_or_run) [reduces via run.grouping mirroring TimeFBAsymmetry, default window = good-bin range]. Field-scan: build_field_scan(runs,order_key) -> FieldScan(sorted x/value/error + excluded list), order_key in {field,temperature,run}; runs missing the log are skipped with a reason (Mantid parity). differentiate_scan(scan,max_gap) = WiMDA dA/dB. 'integral' path reuses compute_asymmetry so integral + time-domain share ONE error model (Mantid-compatible); alpha=1.0 reproduces WiMDA exactly. LAYERING: transform takes NO io dependency - period selection is upstream (periods.select_period). Validated end-to-end on corpus EMU LF series 10-100 G @350 K (repolarisation recovery curve). DEFERRED follow-ups: GUI scan representation + trend panel, .asymp persistence as a representation type, and ALC baseline+peak-fit (alc-avoided-level-crossing candidate, depends on this). ---- STUDY: Flagged by WiMDA Muon School corpus testing: unblocks ALC/repolarisation/QLCR. Two-layer feature: (1) per-run reduction counts/asymmetry over [t_min,t_max] -> one (value,error) scalar; (2) series assembly across runs ordered by field/temperature/run -> scan curve. FINDINGS: WiMDA ALC mode = count-integral (F-B)/(F+B) over full good-bin window, NO alpha, Poisson error, plus dA/dB differential transform (muondata.pas:807-815, FitTableUnit.pas:322-402). Mantid PlotAsymmetryByLogValue is the most complete + most portable reference: alpha-aware, two Types - Integral (integrate counts THEN form (F-aB)/(F+aB)) and Differential (form A(t) per bin THEN integrate) - single [TimeMin,TimeMax], Red/Green periods, LogValue sample-log x-axis + Function(Mean/Min/Max/First/Last); feeds 3-step ALC interface (load->baseline->peak fit). musrfit NEGATIVE RESULT: no native integral observable; ALC is just a filename instrument tag fitted as ordinary fittype-2 asymmetry; mupp plots FITTED params vs field, never integrates counts. RECOMMENDATION (Option A): port Mantid model as behavioural contract with WiMDA count-integral as default Integral formula (alpha=1.0 reproduces WiMDA exactly); reject musrfit param-only approach for core observable. Realise as Qt-free core/transform/integral.py reusing compute_asymmetry kernel (shared Mantid-compatible error model) + good-bin windowing + periods.py red/green, plus a FIELD_SCAN_INTEGRAL representation/series reusing FitSeries order_key + fit_parameters_panel trend surface. Mantid GPL-3 = oracle only, do NOT vendor (same rule as maxent study). ALC baseline-subtraction + peak-fitting DEFERRED to follow-up alc-avoided-level-crossing candidate (depends on this). Asymmetry already has every primitive (alpha-aware asymmetry, good-bin window, period selection, field/T metadata, series ordering, trend plot) - port is mostly composition.

UMBRELLA study, now PROGRAMME COMPLETE (2026-06-13) — see programme-complete.md for the authoritative final status + full PR ledger (#29-#75). Full WiMDA source sweep (every unit in wimda.dpr) vs fresh Asymmetry scan at 474534e; supersedes the WiMDA column of comparison-matrix.md. Delivered: 12-project portfolio across Wave A (#30-#44) + collision reconciliation (#46-#51) + Wave B (#54-#58) + post-Wave-B follow-on tranche (#69/#71/#72/#73/#75), plus emergent projects (exact-Poisson error #35, radical correlation #43) and the API-only negmu WIP port (#69). Functional parity complete; everything unshipped is trigger-parked by design (follow-on-triage.md DEFER tier). Decisions: Burg all-poles MEM INCLUDED as diagnostic; Eigen.pas/Kramers-Kronig/HDF4/MUD permanently out (see decision-record.md). Physics: the MuoniumLFRelax (1-delta) prefactor was checked against Kadono and removed as spurious (#71). WiMDA-bug ledger (KTBArray low-field, General-alpha no-interior-minimum, ZFdipgen J>1/2, etc.) in decision-record.md section 3 = standing 'do not oracle against' list.

Parameter-trending model functions + Model-layer machinery (umbrella wimda-parity-gap, Wave A). All 8 fitfunctions.pas functions and the Model.pas machinery verified against the Pascal source. Phase 1: Polynomial, PowerLawQuadBG, MuRepolarisation (A_hf-parameterised, B0=A/(gamma_e+gamma_mu) from constants.py) + documented composite recipes (Arrhenius+Arrhenius eV->meV delta 0.089%; OrderParameter+Constant; 2xLorentzianLCR+Polynomial with coefficient non-transfer warning). Phase 2: error-mode selector (Column/Percent/Absolute/None + estimate-from-scatter as the one-pass fixed point of WiMDA's Estimate iteration), union multi-range windows on ModelFitRange, shared chi2-quality helper (core/fitting/fit_quality.py, reused by fit-workflow-diagnostics). Session-only (no .asymp changes); GUI in ModelFitDialog only. OUT: x2 second variable (requirements recorded; cross-group fitting covers it), *fit.dll (-> python-user-functions), .mfit ReloadFit, GLE. IMPLEMENTED 2026-06-10: Phases 1+2 shipped (validate green, 1810 passed); EuO regression Tc=69.24(9)/beta=0.409(7)/alpha=1.18(4) matches PR #15 within combined sigma; arbitrary-X stretch not reached (follow-on; GUI-only, core is x-agnostic).

studies.feature_name: Data-reduction parity (alpha estimation, backgrounds, binning modes, t0 search, detector exclusion, period mapping)
studies.notes: Wave A project 1 of the wimda-parity-gap umbrella. Three phases: (1) diamagnetic + General alpha estimators with uncertainties; (2) tail-fit background for pulsed sources + frame-ratio background-run subtraction; (3) variable/constant-error binning, automatic t0 search, per-detector exclusion, multi-period subset->red/green mapping. Phase 3 period mapping realises the REMAINDER of the period-arithmetic candidate (period-selection shipped the red/green selection slice; this adds arbitrary subset summation with Ignore). WiMDA arithmetic transcribed as test oracles; divergences recorded in comparison.md.

studies.feature_name: Model-fit follow-ons (arbitrary X, cross-group error/windows, results-table recursion)
studies.notes: Second-pass Model-layer follow-ons from model-function-parity (PR #32). Item 1 arbitrary X: any fitted parameter as x (param:<name> encoding), common-scope components (core component_names_for_x already degrades), opt-in effective-variance (Orear/York) x-uncertainty toggle default-off + horizontal x-bars (ODR rejected: no box bounds). Item 2 cross-group error modes + windows: global_fit_parameter_model gains error_mode/error_value/windows (reuse apply_error_mode/windows_mask; SCATTER rescales global+local sigma) FIRST, then un-hide CrossGroupFitDialog controls + config persistence. Item 3 results-table recursion: cross-group local+global outputs become a new trendable _GroupFitData series in FitParametersPanel (single-fit-range export deferred). Item 4 STRETCH: quadrature combinator. Decisions A/B/C agreed 2026-06-10. WiMDA columnscan/column-spinners verified directly against Model.pas. Study pass only; implementation paused for go-ahead. IMPLEMENTED 2026-06-10: items 1-3 shipped, validate green. Phase 3 landed as a first-class computed FitSeries in ProjectModel (MainWindow._record_model_fit_results_series) rather than panel-injected rows (load_representation_series rebuilds from ProjectModel, so a panel-only series would be wiped); no _FitRow.origin needed. Item 4 (quadrature combinator) remains a follow-on.

studies.feature_name: Count-domain fit modes (α-free F+B, single-histogram, exclude range, fittable t0/baseline, count-loss in fit, double pulse)
studies.notes: Wave A project 3 of wimda-parity-gap. IMPLEMENTED 2026-06-10, all 3 phases validate-green (P1 2126 / P2 2135 / P3 2145). Raw-count fits with selectable Poisson(Cash, default)/Gaussian cost. P1: build_fb_count_model (sqrt(alpha) F/B tie) + count_domain.py driver; single-histogram = one-group degenerate case of build_grouped_count_model; raw model = e^(-t/tau)x existing builder; engine.py untouched. P2: interior exclude window, fittable t0, baseline drift (all no-op-when-off). P3: count-loss DT0(+C2/C3/C4 polynomial) + promote_deadtime_to_grouping (Send-to-Group, additive/replace) + double-pulse single-histogram (simulate_double_pulse_run round-trips dpsep; dpsep FIXED from metadata - non-smooth gate defeats migrad). GUI: fit-target selector in MultiGroupFitWindow + minimal fit_panel.py routing. Follow-ons: unify fgAll onto Poisson, free tau, DT1/power-law deadtime, robust dpsep scan, FB double-pulse, plot overlay, MINOS-on-alpha. NOT pushed.

studies.feature_name: MaxEnt completion: reconstruction overlay, ISIS pulse shape, ZF/LF+SpecBG, deadtime/phase calibration
studies.notes: Wave A project 6 of wimda-parity-gap umbrella; EXTENDS docs/porting/maxent/ (the implemented-engine study) without re-litigating it. Implements that study's deferred Phase1/2/follow-on items: (1) time-domain reconstruction overlay (opus already computes it); (2) ISIS pulse-shape response in the forward model (single+double, parabolic proton FT x pion Lorentzian); (3) ZF/LF 2-group F/B mode + SpecBG zero-freq pseudo-Voigt subtraction; (4) deadtime fitting (DEADFIT) + editable phase/amp/deadtime tables + fitted-phase exchange with grouped time-domain fits (absorbs phase-auto-calibration WiMDA slice). KEY FINDING: shipped engine is a projected-gradient V1 with REAL cosine/sine forward-adjoint, NOT WiMDA's complex-FFT Skilling-Bryan kernel -> all new physics folds into that contract via the existing cos/sin component split, no rewrite. Scope decisions with Ben 2026-06-10: synthetic-only pulse validation (single+double); suggest-only deadtime promotion; strict 2-group ZF/LF parity; overlay as plot-workspace view token; core/fourier/units.py simple converters (shared w/ frequency-domain-finishers, API recorded); ZF/LF mode selector hard-constrains group table; NO schema bump (from_dict defaults; signature-only). Looseness/phase-accel knobs verdict OUT (target WiMDA's SB kernel V1 doesn't run). Sconv deferred (numerical hazard). Mantid not importable; MaxentTools pure-numpy kernels = optional skipif oracle, copy nothing. IMPLEMENTED in 3 phases (validate green each): P1 reconstruction overlay (TIME_MAXENT_RECON rep + reconstruction view token + residuals renderer); P2 ISIS pulse-shape response folded into the real cos/sin kernel as R(nu)cos(theta-delta) + interior exclusion sigma-inflation + core/fourier/units.py MHz/Gauss/Tesla helper (shared w/ frequency-domain-finishers); P3 ZF/LF 2-group alpha-tie + SpecBG display subtraction + spectrum/log export + phase exchange (rad/deg by group id, via fit_panel grouped seed bridge) + deadtime fit reusing calibrate_deadtime_from_histograms (suggest-only promotion). Divergences in comparison.md.

studies.feature_name: Frequency-domain finishers: field-axis units, exclusion ranges, pulse-rolloff compensation, robust baseline, S/N readouts, real+imag, Burg pole scan, diamagnetic removal
studies.notes: Wave A project 5 of wimda-parity-gap umbrella. IMPLEMENTED (both phases, validate green). Phase 1: post-FFT conditioning in core/fourier/conditioning.py (pulse comp = 1/R(nu) from maxent/pulse.py capped+node-cutoff, NOT WiMDA unbounded Gaussian; iterative sigma-clip baseline, WiMDA single-pass=1 iter; exclusions wire exclude_frequency_ranges + diamag slot + PSI preset; Real+Imag overlay; S/N readout). Phase 2: core/fourier/burg.py all-poles diagnostic (memcof/evlmem/FPE, display mode 'Resolution (Burg)') + core/fourier/diamag.py time-domain fit-and-subtract (field write-back + time overlay). Field-axis verify-only (plot_panel FieldUnit). Deferred: radical correlation spectrum, N0 single-histogram.

studies.feature_name: Muoniated-radical correlation spectrum (WiMDA Corr/AvCorr + rmatch Breit–Rabi pair-matching)
references: McKenzie, Annu. Rep. Prog. Chem. Sect. C 109, 65 (2013)
Blundell et al., Nat. Rev. Methods Primers 1, 89 (2021)
studies.notes: Promotion of frequency-domain-finishers comparison.md §9 (deferred follow-on). Verified vs WiMDA Pascal (Plot.pas rmatch 515-523, w12/w34 525-551, CorrFn 1387-1394, Corr/AvCorr loop 2149-2230; FFTPar CorrField/CorrOrder). Physics reconciled across THREE sources to machine precision: textbook eqn 4.54/4.65 (A=nu12+nu34 high-TF) == McKenzie eqns 6-8 (A_mu=nu43-nu12, her nu12 signed-negative) == muonium.py _tf_levels w12+w34. Decisions: exact Breit-Rabi forward map via muonium.py (NOT rmatch's approximate inverse w/ 5th-sig-fig rounded constants); build as derived display mode through compute_average_group_spectrum like Burg; badged specialist radio; A_mu coupling axis (MHz) excluded from field-unit selector; reference-field + CorrOrder(=2) controls; correlate-the-averaged-spectrum (AvCorr-like, documented divergence). Synthetic-first verification (cyclohexadienyl A_mu=514.4(1) MHz). User docs MUST teach radical-uSR + TF-vs-ALC complementarity (ALC already shipped PR #23, NOT touched). IMPLEMENTED (3 phases): core/fourier/correlation.py + spectrum.py derived mode + fft.py alias (P1); badged GUI radio + reference-field/order controls + Aμ-axis field-unit lock + schema recipe keys (P2); user-guide page docs/user_guide/radical_correlation.rst with TF-vs-ALC subsection (P3). validate green 2328 passed.

studies.feature_name: Fit-workflow diagnostics: MINOS asymmetric errors, χ² quality band, chain-seeding, mid-fit abort, persistent fit record
studies.notes: Wave B. Absorbs the minos-error-analysis candidate. MINOS=display-only overlay (additive FitResult.minos_errors + 'uncertainties_asymmetric' summary key; downstream surfaces stay symmetric HESSE by design). One shared engine.py drive helper (migrad + opt-in MINOS; explicit HESSE only pre-MINOS on simplex since migrad already runs HESSE) from all three minimiser sites (FitEngine.fit, global_fit, count_domain._solve). fit_quality already an EXACT WiMDA port (chi2.cdf==Gammp); wired through result_summary + every surface (W7 'quality' key), R fixed 0.95. Chain-seeding ('chain from previous run', distinct from warm_start_source) passes through normalize_to_grouped_contract (W5); Auto order-key default. Abort mirrors MaxEnt contract: core cancel_callback + FitCancelledError, in-fit raise + between-member checks, nothing recorded on abort (W6). FitLog REFRAMED (Ben 2026-06-12): no append-file; .asymp already stores latest-fit-per-dataset (FitSlot.result) + latest batch (FitSeries.results_by_run) = WiMDA's overwrite .fit/.bfit snapshots; enrich those records additively + Qt-free fit_log.py formatter + on-demand export only (W14). Follow-ons: in-batch co-add, fgAll Poisson cost-factory unification, provenance keys at recording sites, worker mixin.
Promoted to full study: see slug 'python-user-functions' (docs/porting/python-user-functions). Candidate docs retained for scoring history; the study supersedes their technical content.

studies.feature_name: Rotating-reference-frame display and fitting (WiMDA RRF + complex-demodulation upgrade)
studies.notes: Promotion of the rrf-transform candidate via the wimda-parity-gap umbrella (Wave B project 10). Verified vs WiMDA Pascal (PlotPar.pas RRF controls 427-475; Plot.pas 2cos+box demod 1652-1695, single-group variant 1712-1727, 2pi/deg conversions 2532-2536; Analyse.pas MusrFun shift 1124-1140 + AsymFitFunction.pas 440-470; dofit raw-data assembly 805-867), Mantid RRFMuon (exact quadrature rotation oracle), musrfit PRunAsymmetryRRF (2cos + packing). Display = complex demodulation + windowed-sinc FIR (WiMDA 2cos+box as named comparison mode; 2w-image suppression is the headline divergence); fitting = core-only frequency-offset CompositeModel wrapper on raw data (textbook: RRF is a visualization tool, not a fit preprocessor). Controls only on fb_asymmetry view; plot_state['rrf'] additive, no schema bump. IMPLEMENTED: core/transform/rrf.py (Blackman FIR, sampling-aware default bandwidth ducking the folded 2nu0 image; exact error propagation; WiMDA mode bin-for-bin vs Pascal loop) + core/fitting/rrf_offset.py (registry+wrapper, delta-nu semantics, fit-equivalence proven, engine-level frequency_offset flagged follow-up) + gui/widgets/rrf_controls.py (one-line panel hook, display-only transform, frame badge, plot_state['rrf']) + corpus smoke on HAL-9500 8T kappa-ET (F2/F7 near-opposite pair; group-sum washout documented) + user_guide/rotating_frame.rst. validate green 2632.

Wave B proj 2. Verified vs WiMDA muondata.pas:2418-2491 (cosign=-1 subtract, frame/spill+period accumulation, ACC tshift align) and BatchFit.pas:300-324 (in-batch coadd reuses same machinery, OUT of scope here). F9: co-subtract built ON subtract_scaled_counts + resolve_background_reference. W3: scalar event-weighted T/field + temperature_spread/field_spread + nested combination block (combined_from kept). W11: rewire _coadd_datasets/rebuild_combined_dataset onto combine_runs, keep negative ids + combined_datasets schema. W12: per-period sum via periods.sum_period_histograms. W1: no schema bump. KEY DIVERGENCE: WiMDA keeps master run's T/field (no averaging in code); Asymmetry event-weights + records spread. Decisions: ref-run-only co-subtract; first-class combined rows where it just works; per-detector t0 align (apply_grouping_aligned); silent recompute on .asymp load; flat menu action.

SUPERSEDES the deferred brief wimda-parity-gap/projects/negative-muon-analysis.md (promotion with changed scope). API-ONLY, work-in-progress: no GUI/panels/dialogs/menus and NO COMPONENTS/MODELS registration (the μ⁻ models live in a new core/negmu/ package, never inserted via registration.insert_definition, so invisible to the GUI pickers by construction). Adapt-not-port: salvage physics+data from WiMDA NegMuAnalyse.pas, discard form structure (p[213..223] slots, GLE export). KEY REUSE FINDING: count_domain (single exp(-t/τ_μ) envelope) and FitEngine (Gaussian-on-asymmetry) CANNOT fit the multi-τ capture sum Σ N_i exp(-t/τ_i)+bg; new fitter reuses drive_minuit/FitResult/Parameter/ParameterSet and replicates only the ~6-line Cash cost (count_domain's is private + off-limits). Element lifetimes literature-anchored to textbook Table C.1 (= Suzuki/Measday/Roalsvig 1987), NOT WiMDA: caught WiMDA's mystrings 69-vs-67 length mismatch, myelements[66]='Ti' typo for 'Tl', and value divergences (Ne 1.520→1.461, Zn 0.169→0.161, Sr 0.142→0.132, Ba 0.072→0.0949). Decisions (Ben): EVERYTHING phased across multiple Sonnet sessions; τ fixed-at-table by default (freeable); ALSO add α-coupled F+B fit; new core/negmu/ package; add simulate_capture_run to core/simulate.py reusing _sample_and_build_run. Plan = plan.md: 5 phases (1 table+model+single-fit+sim; 2 F+B+ratio; 3 Set-as-BG; 4 μ⁻SR polarisation; 5 docs), full signatures, reuse audit, constants table w/ citations, test plan w/ expected values, WIP disclaimer verbatim, ready-to-paste Phase-1 implementer prompt. Promotion trigger for GUI: real ISIS μ⁻ data + a user. STUDY+PLAN ONLY this session — no src/tests changes.

studies.feature_name: Unified asymmetry projections (vector polarization + TF dual grouping)
studies.notes: Internal unification (not an external port). Vector polarization (EMU P_x/P_y/P_z) and TF dual detector grouping (MuSR/HiFi) are one abstraction: a set of named projections, each a (fwd,bwd,alpha) F/B pair of the same run. Converged design w/ Ben 2026-06-13: (1) first-class AsymmetryProjection on PresetGrouping + schema, retiring magic group-name string-matching in 3 GUI sites (grouping_dialog._detect_vector_axis_pairs, mainwindow._vector_axis_pairs_for_grouping/_vector_axis_state_for_dataset); (2) multi-select projection chip bar replacing the x/y/z/All combo, floor-of-one, NO 'All' chip (redundant in multi-select; optional 'all' text-action open micro-decision), one projection = one stacked subplot, never co-plotted; (3) frame-tint = projection identity vs trace-colour = run identity (RG mode) kept orthogonal; (4) selectable subplots set the fit target (ring+pill, fit-panel echo), requiring projection-keyed FitSlot map (currently (run,rep_type)-only, axis-agnostic; plot panel has transient (run,axis_key) curve caches only) → schema v9 + migration; (5) joint RF fitting deferred but door left open by shaping per-projection storage to mirror FitSeries.results_by_run as results_by_projection. NOT IMPLEMENTED.

ADAPT data-only/ASCII export (strengthen the existing GLE .dat path, content switch, NO second button; WiMDA_Main.pas:1414-1869), snapped cursor readouts FULL SET (snap+S/N + windowed-avg via integrate_curve + 3-pt parabolic peak on the existing cursor signal; Plot.pas:106-186,1159-1228 — belongs to the spectrum-reading workflow), REJECT F,B balance overlay (checkpoint 2026-06-13: estimator suite + alpha-free fit already serve calibration). Checkpoint accepted 2026-06-13. DESIGN-ONLY live current-run monitoring (refreshable-loader hook). No schema bumps; additive panel-state keys only; GPL oracle-only.

references: McKenzie et al., J. Phys. Chem. B 117, 13614 (2013)
studies.notes: Closes parity gap PC1 (RigiWorkshopFit RF-resonance / exact-diagonalisation fit). Ports WiMDA RFresonanceMuPlusProtonExact + muproton1: 8x8 isotropic contact Hamiltonian H = A_mu*Se.Smu + A_p*Se.Sp + (g_e*Sez - g_mu*Smuz - g_p*Spz)*B (MHz, field in G), diagonalised with numpy.linalg.eigvalsh (Eigenuni.pas superseded — Hermitian spectrum is basis-independent, so sorted level differences are identical to machine precision). RF resonance = fields where sorted transitions E7-E5 and E8-E6 (WiMDA selectors 75/86) equal nu_RF; model = BG + two Lorentzians. Implemented as field-scope parameter-trend component RFResonanceMuP in parameter_models.py (params A_mu,A_p,nu_RF,ampl1,wid1,ampl2,wid2,BG). Only the EXACT variant is registered; the analytic Breit-Rabi variant (kept as analytic_rf_transition_freqs for cross-check) under-splits at low field. Ap=0 reduces to muonium._tf_levels (validated). VERIFIED end-to-end on the benzene corpus (DEVA runs 56426-56462, nu_RF=218.5 MHz, 293 K): fitting the Green-Red integral-asymmetry field scan recovers A_mu=516.0 MHz (paper 514.78(4)) and A_p=125.4 MHz (paper 124.6(14), within stated sigma), resonances 866/772 G (digitised 865/773), chi2/dof=1.6. A_mu is the robust observable; A_p (the splitting) is the weak axis, as the paper notes. 19 new tests; 185 touched existing tests green.

references: Sonier, Brewer, Kiefl, Rev. Mod. Phys. 72, 769 (2000)
Pratt et al., Phys. Rev. B 79, 052508 (2009)
studies.notes: Adds time-domain vortex-lattice lineshape fit components VortexLattice and VortexLatticePowder: P(t)=A*Re[exp(i(2*pi*gamma_mu*B0*t+phase))*R(t)], R(t)=<exp(i 2pi gamma (B(r)-B0) t)>_r the characteristic function of the modified-London triangular-FLL field distribution p(B) (skewed: sharp low-field cutoff, high-field core tail). Fixes the single-Gaussian underestimate of sigma_VL (window/binning-dependent, 0.7-3.1 on LiFeAs run 3366) by fitting the lineshape directly. Width calibrated to brandt_field_width_sigma[_powder] so lambda/Bc2 match the SC_Brandt_VortexLattice trend models; modified-London supplies only the shape. Params [A, field, phase, lambda_ab, Bc2], field pre-fixed; powder uses 3^(1/4) lambda_ab. Core sc/lineshape.py (cached FLL build); 12 tests (anchor, skew, R(0), degenerate, lambda^-2, round-trip lambda=195.1+/-0.1 nm). LiFeAs powder headline remains data-degeneracy-limited (fast VL + slow nuclear + Ag background overlap); documented in the wimda-eval cookbook.

studies.feature_name: Integral (ALC) field-scan representation in the main window plot area
references: Mantid MuonALC / ALCInterface (integrated-asymmetry field scan as primary plot)
studies.notes: GUI-only design pass (no code written). Goal: move the integral (ALC) field scan from the right-hand ALCScanView (Parameters dock) into the central plot area, since the per-run time spectra are only integrated in scan mode. Core (FieldScan/build_field_scan/fit_scan_baseline/fit_scan_model) and FitSeries.extra persistence (schema v10) stay untouched. Central plumbing: PlotWorkspacePanel stack holds only _time_panel/_frequency_panel and integral_scan normalizes to fb_asymmetry (plot_panel.py ~1606); dock swap via _alc_mode/_sync_fit_dock_mode (mainwindow.py ~4920-4972). Crux design tension: the integration window IS the draggable time-plot fit-range, so removing the time spectra collides with window selection. See implementation-options.md and handoff.md; six design questions to resolve with the user before implementing.

studies.feature_name: Fit / trend / representation consolidation (cross-representation fit + parameter-trending consistency: F21 round-trip, series identity/naming, quality gating, DataGroup<->FitSeries linking)
references: WiMDA Muon School corpus (ISIS EMU teaching data)
studies.notes: Study + phased plan complete (README, comparison F1-F22/D1-D8, IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN 8 phases). Phase 0.1 (schema v10->v11 keyed fit_states, F21c) implemented.

studies.feature_name: Global fit wizard efficiency (effort-level slider Low/Balanced/Thorough/Exhaustive: exact bounds, escalation-on-anomaly, Q pre-tests, greedy/surrogate search, profiled locals + variable projection)
studies.notes: Study + 5-PR phased plan complete, decision-ready. PR1 golden-verdict harness (gate, <10min via frozen-baseline cache + P<=5 cases), PR2 free wins (layer/cross-template bounds + no-Hesse screening + escalation-on-anomaly, target 7-12x, all tiers), PR3 engine G-scaling (profiled/nested locals + VarPro, Ben-requested, own PR), PR4 search+surrogate (greedy _search_parameter_roles + Q pre-tests + Wald surrogate + template racing), PR5 effort slider + GUI (Sonnet). Spine PR1->PR2->PR4->PR5; PR2//PR3 parallel (share only fit_global sig, mitigate w/ stub commit). Dropped: per-node B&B (redundant w/ layer bound), _staged_globalization as search, GlobalSearchOrchestrator on slider (park behind post-delivery bake-off), G subsetting, simplex-first.

PSI's 2026 FLAME DAQ writes MusrRoot RunHeader as nested TDirectorys of labelled, globally-numbered TObjString leaves ('NNN - Label: Value -@type'), per musrfit's 2025 TDirectory commit series (e38fa479 skeleton, 7e28402e infrastructure, 4917e5c7 reader, ea646e01 MusrRoot v2.0, df7b8433 PRunDataHandler::ReadRootFile TDirectory+TFolder, dbbaf554 unrelated XML-schema DOI move). RootLoader already detected the TDirectory layout but never decoded the per-leaf encoding, so such files loaded with blank title, 0.0 temperature/field, a filename-guessed run number, and verbose histogram-title detector labels. Fix decodes each leaf with the existing _parse_musrroot_string regex (verified to match musrfit's first-'-'/first-':'/last-' -@' delimiter semantics, internal hyphens preserved), keyed on label (NNN is a single counter shared across all subfolders, so numbering is non-contiguous per subfolder and must never be used as a key); encoded-vs-clean discrimination uses the TKey name, since clean payloads like dates false-parse. Asymmetry-specific divergences: RunSummary (free text; musrfit ignores this block — its ring-anode-HV mining reads the legacy LEM run summary, not MusrRoot's) is attached verbatim as metadata['musrroot_run_summary'] provenance rather than discarded; instrument matching is now case-insensitive (FLAME writes lowercase 'flame'); a backward-compatible 'Label: Value' clean-form fallback is accepted for synthetic fixtures. Legacy TFolder MusrRoot and pre-2011 LEM ROOT are unaffected. Validation is synthetic encoded fixtures (no real PSI data committed); a 2026 FLAME commissioning-era file was checked locally only, and no path/filename/run number/sample/proposal detail from it appears anywhere in this repository.

studies.feature_name: Correction order vs. alpha estimation (deadtime + background before the wTF alpha estimate; unified corrected preview)
studies.notes: Physics bug + WiMDA fidelity divergence: alpha is estimated on raw grouped counts while the reduction applies deadtime+background first, so a calibrated alpha does not centre the background-subtracted wTF asymmetry. Approved direction (domain consult + reference cross-check): estimate alpha on deadtime-corrected -> t0-aligned -> grouped -> background-subtracted counts (one step before binned_fb_asymmetry), for all three methods; reference-run background pre-corrected before scaling. UI direction: unify to a single corrected-reduction preview (Corrections panel) with staleness badge, retiring the deadtime/background/alpha modals.

studies.feature_name: Beta correction (musrfit asymmetry fit type 2: intrinsic detector-asymmetry balance)
studies.notes: Port of musrfit's beta (A0_b/A0_f intrinsic-asymmetry balance) applied with alpha in one formula: ours A=(F-alphaB)/(betaF+alphaB), beta identical in both conventions. Determination is independent of alpha (beta invisible to count-ratio estimators; beta does not move the corrected asymmetry's zero), so the GUI gets a separate blue Corrections card. v1: fixed user-entered scalar only, default 1, key emitted only when != 1 (no schema bump); exact Poisson error generalizes 2|a| -> |a|(1+b) with denominator betaF+alphaB. Deferred pending expert consult: data-driven estimator == fittable beta in the count-domain FB model; per-projection vector beta.
